channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Petrol loading resumes as depot prices climb
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Fuel marketers resume petrol loading from private depots despite climbing depot prices, with uncertainty surrounding Dangote Refinery’s dollar-for-fuel pol
Read More: https://punchng.com/petrol-loading-resumes-as-depot-prices-climb/

item.title: Joshua motivated to continue boxing, says Hearn
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Anthony Joshua is motivated to extend his boxing career beyond earlier retirement plans, according to Matchroom chairman Barry Hearn. He now plans to fight
Read More: https://punchng.com/joshua-motivated-to-continue-boxing-says-hearn/

item.title: Govt promotes equipment leasing to boost youth agribusiness
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Federal Government promotes equipment leasing to empower young Nigerians in agribusiness, boosting food security and agricultural productivity. Learn more.
Read More: https://punchng.com/govt-promotes-equipment-leasing-to-boost-youth-agribusiness/

item.title: Financial stability remains foundation for growth – Oyedele
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Minister Taiwo Oyedele emphasizes financial stability as the bedrock for Nigeria’s economic growth, highlighting public trust and crisis preparedness.
Read More: https://punchng.com/financial-stability-remains-foundation-for-growth-oyedele/

item.title: NUPRC awards 37 oil blocks, warns against delays
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NUPRC has awarded 37 oil and gas blocks to 31 companies, warning winners against delays in meeting post-award conditions. Learn more about the 2025 Lic
Read More: https://punchng.com/nuprc-awards-37-oil-blocks-warns-against-delays/

item.title: FG settles N333bn GenCos debts
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has settled N333bn of GenCos debts, fulfilling commitments under its power sector debt financing programme and restoring investor co
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-settles-n333bn-gencos-debts/

item.title: Banks’ profits surge 12‑fold since Soludo’s 2005 recapitalisation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian banks have seen profits surge 12-fold since Soludo’s 2005 recapitalisation. Discover how major banks like Zenith, GTB, and Access Bank have grown.
Read More: https://punchng.com/banks-profits-surge-12%e2%80%91fold-since-soludos-2005-recapitalisation/

item.title: OPay unveils tech engine, eyes one billion users
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
OPay reveals its advanced tech engine, achieving 99.9% transaction success and aiming for one billion users. Discover OPay’s commitment to financial inclus
Read More: https://punchng.com/opay-unveils-tech-engine-eyes-one-billion-users/

item.title: MfB unveils match fund for women entrepreneurs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Happy Woman Match Fund, in partnership with Mayden Microfinance Bank, offers financing to women entrepreneurs for business tools, bridging capital access g
Read More: https://punchng.com/mfb-unveils-match-fund-for-women-entrepreneurs/

item.title: Businesses urged to upgrade for digital tax era
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria is moving to a digital tax system with e-invoicing. Businesses must upgrade processes to ensure compliance and avoid issues with tax claims.
Read More: https://punchng.com/businesses-urged-to-upgrade-for-digital-tax-era/

item.title: 2027: PRP presidential aspirant warns against bloodshed
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The presidential aspirant of the Peoples Redemption Party (PRP) for the 2027 general election, Yakubu Kingsley, has warned that no Nigerian blood should be shed over politics, insisting that no political ambition was worth a Nigerian life. He appealed to fellow presidential aspirants and political l

item.title: 2027: Kogi’ll deliver massive votes to Tinubu – Gov Ododo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Governor of Kogi State, Usman Ododo, has assured that the state electorate would massively give the desired votes to President Bola Tinubu in the next year’s presidential election. The post 2027: Kogi’ll deliver massive votes to Tinubu – Gov Ododo appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: UNICEF, partners launch initiative to prevent child malnutrition in Gombe
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF), in collaboration with the Gombe State Government and the Latter-Day Saints Charities (LDSC), has launched a new initiative aimed at preventing malnutrition during the first 1,000 days of life, describing the period as critical to a child’s survival, growt

item.title: Family Homes hands over 50 houses to families of fallen military personnel in Oyo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Family Homes Funds Limited (FHFL) has formally handed over 50 units of two-bedroom detached, energy-efficient bungalows to the spouses of Nigeria’s fallen military personnel. The post Family Homes hands over 50 houses to families of fallen military personnel in Oyo appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Lagos strengthens business tourism with Wigwe auditorium
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Legend Hotel Airport, Lagos, has unveiled the Herbert Wigwe Auditorium, a state-of-the-art event and conference facility that is redefining meetings and events landscape in Lagos. The post Lagos strengthens business tourism with Wigwe auditorium appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: COREN urges UNN graduates to adhere to engineering profession’s regulations
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria (COREN) has urged maiden-inducted graduates of the Faculty of Engineering in the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) to adhere strictly to regulations of the profession. The post COREN urges UNN graduates to adhere to engineering profession’s

item.title: Adesina emerges Three Crowns mum of the year
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Three Crowns Milk, one of Nigeria’s dairy brands from FrieslandCampina WAMCO Nigeria Plc, has announced Olaide Adesina as winner of the Three Crowns Mum of the Year 2026 at the grand finale of its flagship mum wellness initiative, recently. The post Adesina emerges Three Crowns mum of the year appea

item.title: Okekenweje lauds Tinubu’s approval of Anambra road projects, seeks urgent dredging of River Niger
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The candidate of the New Nigeria Peoples Party (NNPP) for Anambra East and West Federal Constituency in the House of Representatives, Jideovo Okekenweje, has commended President Bola Tinubu for approving the design and procurement of two major federal road projects linking Anambra State with neighbo

item.title: Tinubu mourns Imam Gbajabiamila, describes him as beacon of guidance for Nigeria
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Bola Tinubu, yesterday, paid tribute to the late Imam Tijani Olubodun Shehu Gbajabiamila, describing the veteran Islamic scholar as a unifying force, whose spiritual leadership and community service left an enduring mark on Lagos State and the nation. The post Tinubu mourns Imam Gbajabiami

item.title: Trump announces new tariffs on generic drugs to take effect in 2028 – US politics live
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: President says 100% tariff would be imposed on generic drugs made overseas after 1 August 2028 ‘for a one year period of time, and 200% thereafter’Sign up for US Breaking News emailUS gas prices have continued to rise since the conflict with Iran resumed this month. According to the American Automo

item.title: Team Tinubu 2027 Ogun marks international widows day in Ifo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Team Tinubu 2027 Ogun State Chapter marked the 2026 International Widows Day with an outreach in Ifo Local Government Area, recently, where hundreds of widows received food items to support their households amid current economic challenges. The post Team Tinubu 2027 Ogun marks international wido

item.title: PHP/USD: Philippine Peso Declines to Match Record Low on Higher Oil Prices
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Philippine peso dropped to match the record low as higher oil prices put pressure on the currency.

item.title: ADC, credible alternative for Nigerians —Plateau gov candidate
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE gubernatorial candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Plateau State, General John Sura (rtd), has stated that the ADC is still dedicated to bolstering democracy and offering Nigeria a reliable political alternative in spite of the legal battles it is facing. General Sura (rtd) dism

item.title: High-Risk Bets That Burned Korean Traders Are Making a Comeback
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korea’s deep-pocketed retail investors are flocking to a leveraged instrument that has inflicted severe losses in the past, adding to concerns about abrupt unwinding of wagers as the stock market gyrates.

item.title: Again, Diri tasks Bayelsans on thanksgiving to sustain God’s glory
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: By Aherhoke Okioma Bayelsa State governor, Senator Douye Diri, has, again emphasised the need for people to make thanking God a way of life to continually attract and sustain divine glory in their lives and the state in general. Governor Diri gave the admonition while speaking at the Mid-Year Thanks

item.title: Trump announces 100% tariffs on imported generic pharmaceutical drugs
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced a 100% tariff on imported generic pharmaceutical drugs on Tuesday. The tariff, which aims to bolster US pharmaceutical production, is set to go into effect in August 2028.

item.title: Dimension Capital’s $800M third fund shows the intersection of science and compute is booming
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The four-year-old firm's latest fund is 60% larger than its second vehicle announced 18 months ago.

item.title: Former Biden official can’t see ‘pilot zones’ scheme working in Lebanon
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Gavito: Israel will likely keep pushing limits in Lebanon, making a full withdrawal unlikely amid security concerns.

item.title: Korean Stocks Jump as Brokers Say Margin Unwind Nearing End
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korean stocks jumped, as the unwinding of leveraged bets that helped drive the benchmark nearly 30% below its peak appeared to be nearing its end.

item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: http://hintjens.com/blog:99 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49000600 Points: 8 # Comments: 2

item.title: Leopard caught on camera storming into a store in Rajasthan, India
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A leopard storms into a store in Rajasthan, India, attacking an employee and causing panic.

item.title: NNPCL’s N7.1trn energy security spending faces scrutiny
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: There have been questions over the expenditure of N7.13 trillion by the Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited (NNPCL) on energy security in 2024. The figure was contained in the NNPCL’s 2024 audit report which was released in November 2025. Although the company did not specifically explain wha

item.title: Middle East live: US launches 11th night of Iran strikes as diplomacy falters
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The US military launched an 11th straight night of strikes on Iran early Wednesday, with President Donald Trump saying the US was "not finished" attacking the Middle Eastern nation. The strikes followed Tehran's attacks on Gulf states, and its Houthi allies threatening to blockade Saudi ports. Follo

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Unpacked July 2026: How to watch
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Samsung's next Galaxy Unpacked event is just around the corner, and the company is expected to take the wraps off a bunch of new devices. Based on the rumors and leaks we've seen so far, Samsung's next generation of foldables will likely be the stars of the show. Samsung is expected to show off a [&

item.title: Indian Rupee Rebound Bets Vanish as Soaring Crude Blunts Inflows
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: With oil prices on the ascent again, bets that tens of billions of dollars in fresh capital inflows would sustain a recovery in the Indian rupee have all but vanished.

item.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: The ribbon-cutting celebration of the Gordie Howe International Bridge is no longer taking place after Trump announced a 50% tariff on Canadian goodsA planned joint US-Canada ceremony Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is n

item.title: Flood sweeps away 2 brothers in Katsina
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A torrential rainfall that hit Katsina metropolis on Sunday claimed the lives of two brothers after they were swept away by floodwaters in Janbago community in the state capital. The victims, Muhammad Salisu, 12, and Abdurrahman Salisu, 7, were said to be returning home from a nearby Islamiyya schoo

item.title: 2026 World Cup: La Roja’s deserved victory
item.dc:creator: The Sun Editorial
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup was concluded on Sunday, July 19, when La Roja of Spain lifted the cup for a second time in the history of the Mundial. The country defeated La Albiceleste of Argentina 1-0 in the explosive final played over 120 minutes at the MetLife Stadium, New Jersey. The winning goal was

item.title: Mandela we celebrate versus Mandela we practise
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: EVERY July 18, the world celebrates Nelson Rolihlahla Mandela. We remember the young lawyer who challenged apartheid, the prisoner who spent 27 years behind bars, the statesman who led South Africa’s transition to democracy, and the global symbol of reconciliation whose name has become almost synony

item.description: The House of Representatives Ad Hoc Committee probing the activities of the controversial Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC) yesterday invited the National Security Adviser, Nuhu Ribadu, and Minister of Foreign Affairs, Ambassador Bianca Odumegwu-Ojukwu, to explain actions the

item.title: Labour Party unveils Bintu Konto as VP candidate
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Labour Party (LP) on Tuesday formally unveiled its 2027 vice presidential candidate, Hajia Bintu Konto, as the party’s national leadership expressed firm confidence in its presidential ticket ahead of the general election. The interactive session, held at the party’s national secretariat in Abuj

item.title: 2027: NNPP uploads presidential, 365 NASS candidates, placeholders
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The New Nigeria People’s Party (NNPP) has announced the upload of the name of its presidential candidate, 365 National Assembly candidates and placeholders on the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal. The party also said the process for the governorship and State House of Assembly

item.title: Lawmaker alleges assassination threat as Ondo Assembly crisis worsens
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The crisis rocking the Ondo State House of Assembly took a dramatic turn on Tuesday after a lawmaker alleged that she narrowly escaped death following her probe into alleged financial impropriety involving the leadership of the House and the Ondo State Oil Producing Areas Development Commission (OSO

item.title: 2027: ADC picks Bello Ambarura as Sokoto deputy gov’ship candidate
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Sokoto State has announced Hon. Bello Isah Ambarura, the member representing Illela/Gwadabawa Federal Constituency in the House of Representatives, as its deputy governorship candidate for the 2027 governorship election. The party disclosed this in a statemen

item.title: Months to election, opposition parties still battling crises in Katsina
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Barely seven months to the 2027 governorship election, the race for Katsina State’s top political seat is intensifying. However, while the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC) leverages its incumbency to consolidate power, major opposition parties are struggling to overcome internal crises that t

item.title: APC chair continues visits to NDC chieftains, meets ex-Kano Gov, Gaya
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Professor Nentawe Yilwatda, has continued his series of engagements with opposition figures in Kano, paying a courtesy visit to one of its chieftains, Senator Kabiru Ibrahim Gaya. Senator Gaya, a founding member of the APC, who later join

item.title: Fresh landslide kills one, body found in flood channel during Calabar downpour
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Persistent torrential rains in Cross River State have claimed another life and left several families homeless after a fresh landslide struck Boro Pit in Satellite Town, Calabar, while residents of Calabar South Local Government Area also discovered a lifeless body trapped inside a flooded drainage.

item.title: Plateau lawmaker denies dumping party, vows to reclaim mandate
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The member representing Jos North-North in the Plateau State House of Assembly, Hon. Adamu Aliyu, has dismissed reports alleging that he resigned from the All Progressives Congress (APC), describing the claims as false and the handiwork of political mischief-makers. Addressing journalists on Tuesday

item.title: Super Micro’s stock soars as its margins unexpectedly double
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The AI server maker now expects gross margins to be in the range of 15% to 17% thanks to an improving customer and product mix

item.title: Yohanna Kusau: A quiet champion of primary healthcare in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ayomide Ladipo & Lois Sambo The day begins early at Kabusa Primary Healthcare Centre. Before the morning sun fully settles over the community, the waiting area is already alive with activity. The cries of babies in their mothers’ arms. Pregnant women gently rubbing their growing bellies

item.title: How did Ejiro happen to journalism: A response to “How did Sheriff happen to Delta?”
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By FESTUS OKUBOR Reading Ejiro Idama’s latest offering in Vanguard was like watching a gifted comedian trying very hard to pass for an auditor. The jokes landed. The arithmetic did not. It reminded me of the old Ika saying: “The man who counts another man’s yams with his eyes often forgets to count

item.title: Nelson Mandela and ANC’s many failures, by Ochereome Nnanna

item.description: If you ask anyone, “who is the greatest African of all time?”, the name of Nelson Mandela of South Africa will likely pop up. I took this question to three Artificial Intelligence (AI) platforms: ChatGPT, Gemini and Claude. They all chorused “Dr Nelson Mandela.” While Gemini

item.title: Taking care of the telecoms poor, by Okoh Aihe
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The holy book tells us that the poor will always live with us in our societies. It means you can never wish away the poor even if you are offended by their existence. No magic wand will obliterate the presence of the poor, except concerted efforts to change their status. Apart from individual effort

item.title: Thai Bonds Are Beating Asia and Analysts Predict Further Gains
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Thailand’s bonds have beaten their emerging Asian peers over the past month and analysts predict they will extend gains on favorable supply dynamics and slower inflation.

The development was disclosed in a statement issued by the Deputy

item.title: Thirty-One Firms Emerge Winners Of 37 Oil, Gas Blocks
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: After a keenly contested bidding process, 31 companies have emerged winners of 37 oil and gas blocks at the 2025 Licensing Round which held in Abuja on Tuesday. THE WHISTLER had reported that a total of 143 companies had submitted 200 bids for 37 out of the 50 oil and gas blocks originally put on [&

item.title: Littler hits nine-darter in epic Matchplay win over Aspinall as MVG beaten
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Luke Littler and Nathan Aspinall produced a World Matchplay epic, with the world No 1 hitting a nine-darter on the way to a mesmerising 11-8 win at the Winter Gardens in Blackpool.

item.description: The judge gave the order after Mrs Godwin-Isaac failed to appear in court for the continuation of the criminal proceedings against her. The post Court orders EFCC to arrest Abuja realtor for skipping hearing in land fraud trial appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Spiro targets net zero by 2040, sets ESG benchmark for Africa’s electric mobility industry
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Spiro, Africa’s largest electric mobility company, on Tuesday unveiled its first Sustainability Report, committing to achieve net-zero Scope 1 and read more Spiro targets net zero by 2040, sets ESG benchmark for Africa’s electric mobility industry

item.title: Lagos bets on youth innovation as Aregbe launches renewed hope skills hub
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: As Nigeria grapples with high youth unemployment and the growing demand for digital skills in an increasingly technology-driven economy, the read more Lagos bets on youth innovation as Aregbe launches renewed hope skills hub

item.title: Papers: Man Utd considering bid for PSG's Zaire-Emery
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The top stories and transfer rumours from Wednesday's newspapers...

item.title: Innolight Seeks Up to $7 Billion in Biggest Hong Kong Listing in Years
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s Zhongji Innolight Co. has started taking investor orders for a Hong Kong listing to raise as much as HK$55 billion ($7 billion), the city’s biggest in seven years.

item.title: ASUU Rejects Bayelsa Allowance Package, Insists On CBA Implementation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Academic Staff Union of Universities (ASUU), Niger Delta University (NDU) chapter, has rejected the Bayelsa State Government’s recent approval of a 40 per cent allowance package for public servants, insisting that the measure does not address agreements already reached with university lect

item.title: Lebanese army deploys in ‘pilot zones’ under US-backed plan
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The Lebanese army has begun deploying in three southern towns as part of a US-backed framework agreement with Israel.

item.title: Trump administration attacks US left, alleging Cuba ties: Key takeaways
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US report says the beleaguered government in Havana is the 'connective tissue' of a global 'anti-American coalition'.

item.title: Moldova chooses new prime minister as it pushes for EU membership
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Political newcomer Vasile Tofan inherits Moldova's push to join the European Union by 2030.

item.title: Iraola wants Mac Allister & Alisson to stay with Reds
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Liverpool boss Andoni Iraola wants midfielder Alexis Mac Allister and goalkeeper Alisson to remain at the club.

item.title: Chelsea, Arsenal and Spurs all want Rashford - Wednesday's gossip
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: A trio of Premier League heavyweights all want Marcus Rashford, Arsenal are interested in Aston Villa's Ezri Konsa, and Nottingham Forest look set to sign a new defender.

item.title: FIFA eyes £220m jackpot with luxury world cup rings for wealthy collectors
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The World Cup has long been about lifting football’s most coveted trophy. Now, FIFA is betting that affluent fans will read more FIFA eyes £220m jackpot with luxury world cup rings for wealthy collectors

item.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the diseaseA humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the

item.title: What can Hearts pick from debris of ongoing summer of hurt?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: There's crashing back to Earth with a bang, and there's whatever Hearts fans have been going through since 16 May.
